channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oil Market Adjusts to Oversupply Amid Geopolitical Volatility
item.description: The article highlights the disconnect between geopolitical events, such as tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, and the underlying trends in the oil market. Despite spikes in prices due to attacks ...

item.title: Fact Check: No Evidence Links COVID-19 Vaccines to Premature Death
item.description: The article debunks a false claim circulating online that COVID-19 vaccines cause recipients to die by 2030. It explains that there is no scientific evidence supporting this assertion. Multiple studie...

item.title: Migrants Contest ICE Shooting Account in Houston Incident
item.description: Three men arrested during an immigration operation in Houston that resulted in the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo are disputing the Department of Homeland Security's account of the incident....

item.title: Zanzibar's Ruling Party and Opposition Agree to Reconciliation and Institutional Reforms
item.description: The ruling Chama cha Mapinduzi (CCM) and the main opposition party ACT Wazalendo have signed a joint statement aimed at resolving the political deadlock following the disputed 2025 elections. The agre...

item.title: Bungie Director's Lawsuit With Sony Resolved, Credits Added To Marathon
item.description: Christopher Barrett, a veteran of Bungie, has settled his legal dispute with Sony after being terminated in 2024 amid allegations of misconduct. Barrett claimed Sony's termination was a breach of cont...

item.title: Telstra's network outage highlights leadership challenges during executive absence
item.description: The article discusses how Telstra's technical issues, exacerbated by the absence of CEO Vicki Brady during a critical period, have sparked broader conversations about corporate governance. While polit...

item.title: Baba Talisha updates on daughter's ADHD and dyslexia diagnosis with next steps
item.description: Baba Talisha has shared an update about his daughter, Baby Talisha, revealing she was diagnosed with ADHD and dyslexia following a comprehensive assessment at the Kenya Institute of Special Education ...

item.title: Spar Issues Recall for Multiple Yoghurt Products Due to Spoilage Concerns
item.description: The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has initiated a recall of several Spar yoghurt products nationwide due to a production machine malfunction. The affected items include fat-free, low-fat, double ...

item.title: England's Preparations for World Cup Quarter-Final Against Norway: Key Insights
item.description: England's squad, managed by Thomas Tuchel, faces a critical World Cup quarter-final against Norway, with several key factors influencing the match. Assistant manager Anthony Barry has emphasized the t...

item.title: Phone thefts reported at Calgary Stampede events as police and organizers take action
item.description: Calgary police have confirmed awareness of multiple phone thefts at Stampede grounds, with at least three incidents reported, including two at Nashville North on Saturday and two at the Badlands Music...

item.title: Lake Zone's economy faces insurance gaps despite high GDP contribution
item.description: Tanzania's Lake Zone, a key economic region contributing nearly 20% of the country's GDP, remains vulnerable to financial risks due to low insurance coverage. The region, home to over 14 million peopl...

item.title: Invertebrate of the Year: Honoring Unsung Heroes in Nature
item.description: The article highlights the annual Invertebrate of the Year competition, encouraging readers to nominate deserving species for recognition. It emphasizes the importance of acknowledging creatures often...

item.title: Edo State Police Enforce Movement Restrictions Ahead of LG Elections
item.description: The police have deployed security personnel across all 18 local government areas in Edo State to enforce a movement restriction from midnight to 6 PM on Saturday, July 11, 2026. This measure is part o...

item.title: England WAGs showcase Miami homes ahead of World Cup match
item.description: The article details the lavish beachside residences of England's Women's National Team (WAGs) Ellie Watkins and Megan Pickford in Miami, where they are staying ahead of their World Cup quarter-final m...

item.title: Delhi High Court upholds Rajpal Yadav's conviction in cheque bounce case, reduces jail term to 3 months
item.description: The Delhi High Court has upheld the conviction of Bollywood actor Rajpal Yadav in a cheque bounce case but reduced his jail term from six months to three months. The court rejected his plea for probat...

item.title: Woman Arrested in Japan for Sewing Housemate’s Lips
item.description: A woman has been arrested in Japan after allegedly sewing her housemate's lips shut, according to local authorities. The incident, which occurred in a residential area of Tokyo, has sparked widespread...

item.title: Heat Dome Threatens Records and Fire Risks Across Drought-Stricken Regions
item.description: A powerful heat dome is set to intensify extreme weather conditions across the United States, particularly impacting drought-stricken areas of the Intermountain West and expanding into the northern Pl...

item.title: White House Spokesperson Condemns Trump's False Claims About Walmart Price Cuts
item.description: A White House spokesperson condemned President Donald Trump for falsely claiming credit for Walmart's price cuts, which the company later clarified were unrelated to his administration. Trump boasted ...

item.title: TSC Warns Against Fake Poster Advertising 24,000 JSS Teacher Internship Jobs
item.description: The Teachers Service Commission (TSC) has officially disowned a fake poster circulating online that falsely claimed to offer 24,000 junior secondary school teacher internship positions. The commission...

item.title: Tanzania’s Leaders to Headline Kizimkazi Festival, Highlighting Regional Development
item.description: The ninth annual Kizimkazi Festival in Zanzibar will feature Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan and Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi as key speakers, marking a significant milestone for the ...
